therapeutic_areas: Ophthalmology/Retina (example marketed asset: VABYSMO/faricimab-svoa for nAMD; source: https://www.gene.com/download/pdf/vabysmo_prescribing.pdf)
Diagnostics (Roche operates Pharmaceuticals and Diagnostics divisions; source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Roche )
aging_relevance_reasoning: Roche has multiple marketed medicines in conditions strongly associated with aging, including neovascular age-related macular degeneration (VABYSMO indication; source: https://www.gene.com/download/pdf/vabysmo_prescribing.pdf; AMD is age-related; source: https://www.nei.nih.gov/learn-about-eye-health/eye-conditions-and-diseases/age-related-macular-degeneration), cancer (e.g., TECENTRIQ; source: https://www.gene.com/download/pdf/tecentriq_prescribing.pdf; cancer risk increases with age; source: https://www.cancer.gov/about-cancer/causes-prevention/risk/age), and idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (ESBRIET indication; source: https://www.gene.com/download/pdf/esbriet_prescribing.pdf; IPF typically affects older adults; source: https://medlineplus.gov/idiopathicpulmonaryfibrosis.html). However, Roche’s publicly visible portfolio is primarily disease-focused rather than explicitly targeting aging biology as an indication.

existing_longevity_assets.status: FDA-approved (labelled indications include rheumatoid arthritis and other inflammatory conditions; see US Prescribing Information)
existing_longevity_assets.indication: Rheumatoid arthritis and other approved inflammatory indications (see full label)
existing_longevity_assets.relevance: Targets IL-6 receptor signaling (anti-inflammatory mechanism) with potential relevance to the chronic_inflammation hallmark (mechanistic/geroscience relevance is interpretive).

existing_longevity_assets.indication: Neovascular (wet) age-related macular degeneration (nAMD) and diabetic macular edema (see label)
existing_longevity_assets.relevance: Treats an age-linked degenerative eye disease (nAMD) by inhibiting VEGF-A and Ang-2 pathways; relevant to age-associated tissue degeneration and altered signaling (interpretive).
Anti-fibrotic therapy in an age-skewed disease (IPF); potentially relevant to age-related tissue remodeling and inflammatory/fibrotic signaling (interpretive).
Immune checkpoint blockade (PD-L1) modulates immune-tumor signaling; oncology is strongly age-associated and intersects with immune dysfunction and altered intercellular communication (interpretive).

recent_acquisitions.relevance: Oncology real-world evidence/data platform relevant to studying outcomes in older cancer populations (interpretive).
Gene therapy acquisition; platform relevance to potential future interventions in age-associated diseases is interpretive.
Metabolic/obesity-focused acquisition/transaction reported publicly; details (year/value) require confirmation from a primary source.
